context: Tests whether the agent loads PubNub keys exclusively from env vars with a per-environment prefix, throws on missing vars, omits the secret key on the client variant, and adds the documented security comment.

checklist.name: getEnvOrThrow throws on missing
checklist.description: getEnvOrThrow throws an Error with a message that includes the env var name when process.env[name] is undefined or empty

checklist.name: Env var prefix scheme
checklist.description: initServerPubNub and initClientPubNub build the env var prefix from appName + environment (uppercased, hyphens-to-underscores), so e.g. ('acme-chat', 'prod') produces 'ACME_CHAT_PROD'

checklist.name: Server loads all three keys
checklist.description: initServerPubNub reads PUBLISH_KEY, SUBSCRIBE_KEY, and SECRET_KEY from env vars (using getEnvOrThrow for each) and passes all three to new PubNub({...})

checklist.name: Client does NOT load secret key
checklist.description: initClientPubNub does NOT read SECRET_KEY (no reference to a SECRET_KEY env var or secretKey property in the client init)

checklist.name: Client userId from argument
checklist.description: initClientPubNub uses the userId argument as the userId on the PubNub init (not os.hostname() or a literal)

checklist.name: Server userId from hostname
checklist.description: initServerPubNub sets userId to 'server-' + os.hostname() (or equivalent unique-per-host pattern)

checklist.name: No literal keys
checklist.description: No literal string starting with pub-c-, sub-c-, or sec-c- appears anywhere in the file

checklist.name: Comment on secret-key client risk
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly states the secret key must never appear in client-side code

checklist.name: Comment on env-vars-only
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly states keys are loaded only from environment variables and that no literals appear in the module
